question_title: What will be the value of "30-Day Average SOFR" on 2026-01-14?
summary: The 30-Day Average SOFR has been declining steadily since mid-December 2025 as the rolling 30-day window incorporates lower post-Fed-cut daily SOFR values (~3.64-3.75) replacing higher pre-cut values (~3.90-3.95). As of Jan 2, the value was 3.77354, declining at ~0.013/day but decelerating. By Jan 14, the 30-day window (starting ~Dec 15) will be almost entirely filled with post-cut SOFR values, so the average should converge toward the recent daily SOFR level of ~3.64-3.70. Central estimate is ~3.70 with a narrow uncertainty band reflecting the highly mechanical and predictable nature of this rolling average.

factors.description: 30-day average is mechanically declining as post-Fed-cut daily SOFR values (~3.64-3.75) replace higher pre-cut values in the rolling window. This is highly predictable.
Daily SOFR has stabilized around 3.64-3.70 in early January, giving a clear floor/convergence target for the 30-day average.
Decline rate decelerating from ~0.015/day to ~0.008/day as window fills with post-cut values, suggesting convergence rather than continued rapid decline.
No FOMC meeting scheduled between Jan 2-14, so no policy rate changes expected. Daily SOFR should remain stable.
Year-end repo market effects (Dec 31 SOFR spike to 3.87) could slightly elevate the average, but this is a single day in a 30-day window.
